The wilderness can also be accessed by canoe or kayak. Campers need to plan ahead and ensure they bring adequate supplies for a successful backcountry trip.

description: The Green River Campground is located along the banks of the Green River in a grove of cottonwood trees at an elevation of 4795 (4162 feet). The famous dinosaur quarry, where you can see 150 million year old dinosaur bones still encased in rock is approximately 5 miles (8 km) from the campground. Nearby is the Split Mountain Boat Ramp where river rafters come off the Green River after trips through Dinosaur National Monument's canyons.

description: Campground is open 24 hours a day, all year. Check out time is 11:00 am. Water is limited and typically only available mid-May to mid-September (after the last frost of spring and before the first frost of fall). In the winter, only Loop B is open (has electrical hookups).
name: South Rim Campground

description: The Southwest Walk-in Campground is open seasonally. The campground closes for the season and typically reopens in June. Campground sites are reserved on site in person through Recreation.gov through a Scan and Pay system. Campsites cannot be reserved in advance. The campground operates first-come, first-served. Once snow covers the ground (typically in November), tent sites close. Self-contained camping is permitted in the parking area - please see additional information on Southwest Campground webpage.
name: Southwest Campground - Summer/Fall

description: The Southwest Walk-in Campground is open seasonally. Once snow covers the ground (typically in November), the campground closes for the season and typically reopens in June.
name: Southwest Campground - Winter/Spring

description: The Split Mountain Campground is open all year, all day. Water is only available seasonally.
name: Split Mountain Group Campground

description: Sprague Creek Campground is generally open from late May to late September.
name: Sprague Creek Campground

description: Spring Canyon is open year-round by reservation only. The lower loop remains open year-round while the upper loop is closed November 1 through March 31.
name: Spring Canyon Campground

description: Spring Canyon group sites are open seasonally. Amenities like flush restrooms, water, and the dump station are not available in winter. They become available again when weather and lake water level allow. Group sites are closed on Wednesdays for maintenance.
name: Spring Canyon Group Sites

description: The turn off to Spring Creek Campground is about 20 miles south of Yellville and 15 miles north of Marshall, Arkansas off of Highway 14. The campground is located 3 miles down at the end of County Road 99. The campground has 12 campsites. All 12 sites are open to tents only. It is open year round with no water or electrical amenities. The vault toilet will be open in the winter, but with very limited servicing.
name: Spring Creek Campground

description: Peak Season: April 15 - November 15 || Non-Peak Season: November 16 - April 14
name: Spring Gap Campground

description: This campground is hike in only, and open 24/7. Reservations can be made on reservation.gov
name: Spud Rock Spring

description: Spur 406 Campground is generally open year-round and rarely fills up, except possibly on holiday weekends. It may close on occasion due to high water events. There is a self-service machine on location for paying for your campsite.
name: Spur 406 Campground

description:
St. Mary operates year-round. All sites require reservations during the spring, summer, and fall. A portion of the campground is open during the winter months in primitive status. Reservations are NOT required in the winter.

All sites in St. Mary Campground will be closed to camping from January 1, 2027–June 30, 2027 due to construction.
name: St. Mary Campground

description: Camping is most popular during the Spring and Fall, but the campground stays open all year. It is common for the campground to fill up throughout the year on Fridays and Saturdays. Make reservations as far in advance as possible.
name: Stafford Beach Campground

description: The Stair Falls Campsite is open year-round. Depending on road conditions, it can be accessed from late May-early November by parking at Haskell Gate and hiking approximately 2 miles. In the winter, campers must park at the north entrance and ski or snowshoe to the campsite.
name: Stair Falls Campsite

description: Open seasonally. Staircase Campground will have no flush toilets or potable water available approximately October 15 as it becomes winterized. The campground will close for the winter on November 1st, 2025.
name: Staircase Campground

description: Primitive camping area. Enter and exit at your own risk.
name: Stanton Creek Primitive Camping Area

description: Campground is open and on the reservations system year-round. Sites not reserved may be obtained on a first come-first serve basis.
name: Station Camp Campground

description: Steel Creek Campground is about 3 miles east of Ponca, Arkansas off of Highway 74 and 13 miles west of Jasper, Arkansas. The tent campground has 26 campsites and the horse campground has 14 sites. It is open year round, restroom and water system in the tent campground are closed in the winter (November 15 - March 15).The vault toilet in the tent campground will be open in the winter, but with very limited servicing. RVs are not allowed.
name: Steel Creek Campground

description: The campground is usually closed from October through mid-May.
name: Stevens Creek Campground

description: Campground is open year-round. Water is generally available from mid-April to mid-November.
name: Stickpile Hill Hiker-Biker Overnight (HBO) Campsite

description: Stone Cliff Campground is open year round.
name: Stone Cliff

description: Open from late fall through mid-to-late April. Closed seasonally during extreme heat.
name: Stovepipe Wells Campground

description: All facility hours are subject to change due to weather, road status, or public safety. This page will be updated as much as possible to reflect current and projected hours / dates.
name: Summit Lake North Campground

description: All dates are subject to change due to weather, road status, or public safety. The dates listed here are the earliest date the campground may open and the latest day the campground may close. These dates and recreation.gov will be updated as much as possible to reflect current projected dates.
name: Summit Lake South Campground

description: Sunset Campground is generally open from late May through the week following Labor Day, depending on weather conditions.
name: Sunset Campground

description:
As of March 25, 2026 ONLY Loop A is open. Open April through October, weather dependent. Closed in Winter. See dates in Reservation section.
When open, Sunset Campground is open 24 hours a day. Check out is at 11:30 am. Quiet hours are from 10 pm to 8 am.
name: Sunset Campground

description:
Typically open late fall through mid-to-late April.  All sites are first-come, first-served. However, the NPS reserves the right to reserve sites at any time due to management needs.

Occupy a site and then pay using the automated fee machine using card or head across the street to the visitor center to pay.
name: Sunset Campground

description: Open year round.
name: Sunset View Campground

description: Campground is open year-round. Water is generally available from mid-April to mid-November.
name: Swains Lock Hiker-Biker Overnight (HBO) Campsite

description: Tamarack Flat Campground is typically open from sometime in June through mid-October; reservations required. Each year the opening date for the Tamarack Flat Campground varies depending on the opening of the Tioga Road and related seasonal facility and road work.
name: Tamarack Flat Campground

description: Wilderness Campgrounds may close due to emergency, maintenance work, or resource protection concerns. All wilderness campgrounds require a Wilderness Use Permit to stay overnight, and all campers must use the tent pads.
name: Tejas Wilderness Campground

description: Check-in/out is 11 am. You must have your site vacated by 11 am and begin heading back toward the park entrance. Quiet hours are 10 pm to 6 am. You are welcome to stop at pull-outs alongside the road if you wish to hike, enjoy the views, etc. Teklanika is open in summer only (see exceptions, below, for details).
name: Teklanika River

description: Standard hours. Quiet hours are 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m.
name: Temple Bar Campground

description: Standard hours. Quiet hours are 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m.
name: Temple Bar RV Park (Lake Mead)

description:
Typically open from late fall through mid-April. Sites are first-come, first-served.

Occupy your site and then pay with card using the automated fee machine or head over to Furnace Creek Visitor Center to pay.
name: Texas Springs Campground

description: Thayer Campground is open year round.
name: Thayer Campground

description: Country store and office hours vary by season. Summer hours are May through September.
name: The Colonies RV Travel Park & Country Store

description: Individual sites at The Needles Campground are normally available year-round. Loop A is open year-round. During winter when The Needles is less busy, Loop B is closed. Group campsites are open March 15 through November 15.
name: The Needles Campground

description: The Point Campground is open year round. The Upper Loop is available by reservation only. The Lower Loop is first come, first served.
name: The Point Campground

description: Typically open from mid-April through December, pending snowfall.
name: Thorndike Campground

description: The campground is open from April 16 - October 31 (the park operating season) annually, 24 hours a day.
name: Three Mile Campground

description: Glacier Basin Campground is closed for the 2025/2026 winter camping season. Planning for trips in summer 2026? Reservations can be made at www.recreation.gov, search for Rocky Mountain National Park - Glacier Basin Campground.
name: Timber Creek Campground

description: The campground is open from April 16 - October 31 (the park operating season) annually, 24 hours a day.
name: Todd Harbor Campground

description: The Tomales Bay beaches on which Boat-in Camping is permitted are open year-round, 24 hours a day for those with a current boat-in camping permit. Camping is by permit only. Camping permits must be obtained before starting your trip. Visitors may make online reservations for a permit at this backcountry (i.e., hike-in) campground at Recreation.gov. Print your Recreation.gov confirmation email and be prepared to show it to park rangers while boating or on the beach.
name: Tomales Bay Boat-in Camping

description: The campground is open from April 16 - October 31 (the park operating season) annually, 24 hours a day.
name: Tookers Island Campground

description: The campground is typically only open summer and fall. Checkout time is 10 am. See exceptions for exact dates of operation.
name: Tower Fall Campground

description: Campground is open year-round. Water is generally available from mid-April to mid-November.
name: Town Creek Hiker-Biker Overnight (HBO) Campsite

description: Registration Kiosk 10 am. - 6 pm. Check-in time: 12 pm. Check-out time: 12 pm. Quiet hours: 10 pm. - 8 am.
name: Trailer Village RV Park - South Rim

description: The campground is normally open seasonally from July - September (dates vary).
name: Tuolumne Horse Campsites

description: The campground is normally open seasonally from July - September (dates vary).
name: Tuolumne Horse Campsites

description: The campground is normally open seasonally from July - September (dates vary).
name: Tuolumne Meadows Campground

description: Turkey Run Ridge Group Campground (TRGC) is a wooded camping facility for organized groups of seven or more persons. The campground is open year-round.
name: Turkey Run Ridge Group Campground

description: Campground is open year-round. Water is generally available from mid-April to mid-November.
name: Turtle Run Hiker-Biker Overnight (HBO) Campsite

description: Twelvemile Beach Campground is open mid-May through mid-October. Closed the rest of the year. All campsites require a reservation, which is obtained through www.recreation.gov. See the Camping page on this website for more information.
name: Twelvemile Beach Campground

description: Open 24 hours.
name: Twin Peaks Campground

description:
Two Medicine Campground's summer season is typically from late May to mid-September.   
There is a primitive camping season from mid-September into October, weather permitting.

The Two Medicine Campground will be closed in 2026 due to construction.
name: Two Medicine Campground

description: Two Rivers Campground is located at the confluence of the Jacks Fork and Current Rivers, at the end of V Highway in Shannon County, MO.  Campsites are available year-round, though restroom with flush toilets is unavailable from October 30 - April 14. A vault toilet is available near the boat ramp.
name: Two Rivers Campground

description: The turn off to Tyler Bend Campground is off of Highway 65 about 30 miles south of Harrison and 15 miles north of Marshall, Arkansas. The campground is located near the Buffalo River, beyond the Tyler Bend Visitor Center. There are 28 drive in sites available for RVs or tents. No hookups available. There are 10 walk in tent only sites and 5 group sites. Flush restrooms, showers, and potable water are available. Fees charged March 15 - November 15.
name: Tyler Bend Campground

description: The Upper East Branch Campsite is open year-round. Typically, it can be accessed by vehicle from late May-early November depending on road conditions. In the winter, campers must park at the north entrance and ski or snowshoe to the campsite.
name: Upper East Branch Campsite

description: Upper Lehman Creek Campground is open typically from June through October. This campground closes seasonally after the first snow of the year.
name: Upper Lehman Campground

description: Upper Pines is open all year for tents, RVs (up to 35 ft.), and trailers (up to 24 ft.). Reservations are required year-round. For the winter season, only the first two loops are normally open (about 90 campsites).
name: Upper Pines Campground

description: Upper Scorpion Campground provides primitive camping opportunities within walking distance of the West Fork of the Gila River. The campground is located less than one mile from the Gila Cliff Dwellings National Monument just off NM State Highway 15. Drinking water is available, and tables and grills are provided at select sites. Vault toilets are available on site. Camping is free of charge and first-come, first-served. This campground does not support RVs or Camper Vans.
name: Upper Scorpion Campground

description: Open April through November.
name: Valley View Group Campground

description: open seasonally
name: Village Campground - North Manitou Island

description: Open year-round. Not operated by the National Park Service. Make reservations at www.lakepowell.com
name: Wahweap Campground & RV Park

description: War Ridge Campground is open year round.
name: War Ridge/Backus Mountain Campground

description: Dates based on Snow Mountain, LLC dates. Please see lassenlodging.com/warner-campground/ for most updated information.
name: Warner Valley Campground

description: The campground is open from April 16 - October 31 (the park operating season) annually, 24 hours a day.
name: Washington Creek Campground

description: Backcountry Campsite
name: Wassataquoik Campsite

description: Backcountry lean-to
name: Wassataquoik Lean-to

description: The campground at Watch Hill is opened seasonally. Camping is typically available from at mid-May to mid-October.
name: Watch Hill Campground

description: Watchman Campground is typically open year-round. The D, E, and F Loops may experience seasonal closures due to weather or maintenance needs. Check recreation.gov for current availability. All sites in Watchman Campground require reservations.
name: Watchman Campground

description: Campground reservations will be required for Wawona Campground from April 15 through October 26, 2026. For the remainder of the year, the campground remains partially open on a first-come, first-served basis.
name: Wawona Campground

description: Wawona Horse Camp reservations are required from early to mid-April through early to mid-October. During the rest of the year the campground is closed. Campground reservations are available in blocks of one month at a time, up to five months in advance, on the 15th of each month at 7 am Pacific time.
name: Wawona Horse Camp

description: The campground is open seasonally.
name: Weather Station Campground - South Manitou Island

description: The campground is open from April 16 - October 31 (the park operating season) annually, 24 hours a day.
name: West Chickenbone Campground

description: Wheeler Peak Campground is open in the shoulder season until the first significant snowfall. Reservations are available one day in advance.
name: Wheeler Peak Campground

description: This campground is available via reservation year-round.
name: White Pine Backcountry Campground

description: Only open and accessible in the summer, White River is open late June to early September depending on weather and snow conditions. Situated in a forest along the White River, the campground has good privacy. No public showers are available in the park. During the summer, check-out time is at noon.
name: White River

description: Campground is open year-round. Water is generally available from mid-April to mid-November.
name: White Rock Hiker-Biker Overnight (HBO) Campsite

description: All campgrounds and campsites are subject to closure, but typically campgrounds are open 24 hours a day, everyday. Check in and check out are at noon. Quiet hours are from 10:00 pm to 6:00 am.
name: White Tank Campground

description:
White Wolf Campground typically opens sometime mid-July and closes sometime in September (exact dates vary). The opening date for White Wolf Campground varies depending on the opening of the Tioga Road and related seasonal facility and road work. When open, reservations are required. 

White Wolf Campground will not open in 2024.
name: White Wolf Campground

description: The Wildcat Campground is open year-round, 24 hours a day. Camping is by permit only. Camping permits must be obtained before starting your trip. Visitors may make online reservations for a permit at this backcountry (i.e., hike-in) campground at Recreation.gov. Print your Recreation.gov confirmation email and be prepared to show it to park rangers on the trail and at your campsite. Place printouts of your confirmation email face up on the dashboard of each vehicle remaining overnight at park trailheads.
name: Wildcat Campground

description: Wilderness cabins are open year-round. Sites may be closed for routine maintenance or resource protection. Closures are displayed on rec.gov.
name: Wilderness Cabins

description: Wilderness Campgrounds may close due to emergency, maintenance work, or resource protection concerns. All wilderness campgrounds require a Wilderness Use Permit to stay overnight, and all campers must use the tent pads.
name: Wilderness Ridge Wilderness Campground

description: The Wilderness Road Campground is open year-round with loops A, E, F, G, and O closing seasonal.
name: Wilderness Road Campground

description: Wilderness tent sites are open year-round.
name: Wilderness Tent Sites

description: Open year round. First-come, first-served.
name: Wildrose Campground

description: Standard hours. Quiet hours are 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m.
name: Willow Beach Campground (Lake Mohave)

description: Standard hours. Quiet hours are 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m.
name: Willow Beach RV Park

description: Currently closed until further notice.
name: Wonder Lake

description: The campground is open from April 16 - October 31 (the park operating season) annually, 24 hours a day.
name: Wood Lake Campground

description: Woolum is located 7 miles off of Highway 65 in St. Joe, Arkansas. Follow County Road 374 west out of St. Joe towards Woolum. The first 4 miles is paved road, the last 3 miles is graded gravel road. The campground is an open field with no designated campsites. No picnic tables, fire grates, water, or flush restroom facilities. There is a horse camp section of Woolum available for campers with pack animals.
name: Woolum

description:
Park Open daily from 8:00 am to 8:00 pm.
Trails Open daily from 8:00 am to 8:00 pm.
Park Office Open daily from 8 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.
name: Worthington State Forest

description: Camping is most popular during the Spring and Fall, but the campground stays open all year. It is common for the campground to fill up throughout the year on Fridays and Saturdays. Make reservations as far in advance as possible.
name: Yankee Paradise Wilderness Campsite

description: Yosemite Creek Campground typically opens sometime early July and closes sometime in September (exact dates vary). The opening date for the Yosemite Creek Campground varies depending on the opening of the Tioga Road and related seasonal facility and road work.
name: Yosemite Creek Campground

description:
Memorial Building:
open 9:00 am - 4:30 pm eastern time.

Birthplace Unit Visitor Center and Grounds: 
open 9:00 am - 5:00 pm eastern time.
name: Birthplace Unit

description:
The Boyhood Home Unit at Knob Creek Grounds:
open daily dawn to dusk.

Knob Creek Tavern Visitor Center hours are different than the park unit.
name: Boyhood Home Unit

description: Acadia National Park is open year-round. Check our website for park facilities operating hours, such as Hulls Cove Visitor Center.
name: Acadia National Park

description: The Visitor Center is open 11:00 am to 4:00 pm Mondays through Fridays. The Visitor Center is closed Saturdays and Sundays. The Visitor Center is closed on wintertime federal holidays, except for Presidents Day.
name: Visitor Center

description: The historic homes are closed for the winter season. The historic homes are expected to reopen in late spring/early summer.
name: Historic Homes

description: The park grounds are open daily sunrise to sunset.
name: Park Grounds

description: The African American Civil War Memorial is always open to the public.  There are no Rangers on duty at this site (except for special events). The United States Park Police are on duty 24 hours a day.
name: Always Open

description: INDOOR VISITOR CENTER AND MUSEUM
name: INDOOR VISITOR CENTER AND MUSEUM

description: OUTDOOR MEMORIAL
name: OUTDOOR MEMORIAL

description:
The park's two trails are open from dawn till dusk every day.

Visitor Center features Miocene Mammal fossils, a 12 minute movie, and the James H. Cook Collection,  a unique collection of American Indian artifacts that were gifts to James Cook and his family.
name: Trails

description: Hours vary dependent on trail section.
name: Ala Kahakai

description: Alagnak Wild River is never closed, but access can be difficult during the winter.
name: Alagnak Wild River

description:
Access to Alcatraz Island is via commercial ferry service. For ticket reservation and schedule call (415) 981-7625 or go to www.alcatrazcitycruises.com. The ferry schedule changes seasonally. Alcatraz is closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas and New Years Day. Once on the island you can stay up to the last ferry. 

Be aware that tickets sell out quickly and reservations are recommended. Tickets go on sale 90 days in advance.
name: Alcatraz Island

description: Alibates Visitor Center has museum exhibits, theater, bookstore, and a 7 themed garden with a Monarch Waystation and other native plants. The Alibates Flint Quarries are accessible by guided tour only. Please call for reservations. There is a short self-guided walking trail with an accompanying brochure that lets you see the Canadian River Valley.
name: Alibates Visitor Center Operating Hours

description:
The Visitor Center, Engine House No. 6 Exhibit Shelter and the Lemon House are closed on Wednesdays and Thursdays. Buildings are open Fridays through Tuesday from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.

Park grounds and trails are open daily from sunrise to sunset, weather permitting.

Park buildings are closed on Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day.

Times listed in "All Park Hours" reflect building operations.
name: Allegheny Portage Railroad National Historic Site

description: The site is currently open to the public with limited hours and services. The site currently consists of a cemetery, a monument, building foundations, and landscaping. Interpretive panels and a downloadable self-guided auto tour are available on amache.org to assist with navigation of the site. There is no visitor center at the site.
name: Amache National Historic Site

description: All public parks, public pathways, and public beaches are open for recreational activities and social gatherings. For visitor center hours, please reference visitor center information tab.
name: American Memorial Park grounds

description: Amistad National Recreation Area is open throughout the year. However, some areas may experience temporary closures because of low water levels. Please see our Operation Hours and Seasons and Current Conditions pages for more specific details.
name: Amistad National Recreation Area

description: Open: 6:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. daily
name: Anacostia Park

description: Open daily 6 am–10 pm. Free skate rentals vary by season — see rental booth hours for details.
name: Anacostia Park Skating Pavilion

description: Free Skate Rental: Memorial Day through Labor Day, Sunday–Wednesday 9 am–5 pm and Thursday–Saturday 9 am–8 pm. September 2–October 31, 2025, rentals will be available Tuesday–Sunday 9 am–5 pm (closed Mondays). Beginning November 1, 2025, rentals will be closed until Memorial Day, Monday, May 25, 2026. Last skate rentals are distributed 30 minutes before closing.
name: Anacostia Park Skating Pavilion: Skate Rental Booth

description: The National Prisoner of War Museum is open daily from 9:30 am to 4:30 pm. Park grounds, including the National Cemetery, are open daily from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. On Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day, the National Prisoner of War Museum and prison site are closed but Andersonville National Cemetery is open from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM pending staff availability.
name: Andersonville National Historic Site Operating Hours

description:
Summer Hours: Generally open daily from 9 am to 4pm 

Winter/Spring Hours: Generally open Wednesday through Sunday from 9 am to 4 pm.

The historic Andrew Johnson Homestead, 209 South Main Street may be open by guided tour at 10:00, 11:00, 2:00 & 3:00, based on staffing. Tours are limited to 10. Tickets are available at the Visitor Center until 15 minutes prior to tour time. Check with Visitor Center desk for availability, as schedule modifications are sometimes necessary.
name: Andrew Johnson National Historic Site

description: Aniakchak National Monument is a truly primitive environment with no federally-maintained public facilities. The NPS imposes no operating hours or seasonal restrictions. Access to and movement within Aniachak, however, may be limited or restricted at any time depending upon prevailing weather conditions.
name: Aniakchak National Monument and Preserve

description: The visitor center is open every day except Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day.
name: Antietam National Battlefield Visitor Center

description: The park is open 24/7 year-round. Visitor centers are open only in summer, with varying hours.
name: Entire Park

description:
In general, the Appalachian Trail is open year-round. The northern terminus at Mount Katahdin in Maine is within Baxter State Park, which may be closed in winter months, depending on weather conditions. 
Particular sections of the Trail, and less-developed roads accessing the Trail, may be closed temporarily for a number of reasons, but otherwise the trail is open.
name: Appalachian National Scenic Trail

description: The park is open daily from 9-5, with the exception of certain holidays: Thanksgiving Day, December 25, and January 1. The park may have reduced hours (9-3) on December 24.
name: Appomattox Court House National Historical Park

description: Arches National Park is generally open 24 hours a day, year-round. Fees apply. The park is very busy between March and October. To avoid traffic, we recommend entering the park before 8 am or after 3 pm.
name: Park Hours

description: The park grounds are open daily: 7:00 am to sunset. The visitor center is open Thursday through Monday: 8:30 am to 5 pm. Please visit the Visitor Center section on this page for more information.
name: Grounds Hours

description:
Arlington House and grounds, the museum, and the Enslaved People's Quarters-North and the Enslaved People's Quarters-South are open daily from 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. Last entry into the plantation house is 4:00 p.m.

The bookstore is open from 9:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. but closes daily for a half hour lunch.

Closed on New Year's Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day.
name: Arlington House

description: Open year round, 24 hours a day.
name: Assateague Island National Seashore - Maryland District

description:
Open January-March 8: 6 am - 6 pm.
March 9-April: 6 am - 8 pm.
May-September: 5 am - 10 pm.
October: 6 am - 8 pm.
November-December: 6 am - 6 pm.
Check with the Chincoteague National Wildlife Refuge (757-336-6122) for updates to operating hours.
name: Assateague Island National Seashore - Virginia District

description: Aztec Ruins National Monument is open to the public from 7:00am-5:00pm, which includes the parking areas, archeological sites, picnic areas, and trails. The visitor center is open from 9:00am-5:00pm daily. The monument and visitor center close on Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Days. Unless there is a public program scheduled, there is no after-hours access. Vehicles are not permitted to be left in the parking lot after closing.
name: Hours

description: The park is open to visitors all year with the exception of weather closures.
name: Badlands National Park

description: The Baltimore-Washington Parkway is generally open year round, 24 hours a day. Check current conditions for information about closures due to road work and inclement weather.
name: Baltimore-Washington Parkway

description: Bandelier National Monument is open every day from dawn to dusk unless impacted by severe weather or noted for specific holidays. The hours of dawn and dusk vary throughout the year so please come prepared knowing when you may have to exit the canyon. Visitors holding an overnight backcountry permit are allowed to be in the park after these hours following the guidance of their permit. The Juniper and Ponderosa campgrounds are open 24hrs.
name: Bandelier National Monument

description: Tsankawi is located 14.5 miles NE of Bandelier National Monument. It is located just outside of White Rock.
name: Tsankawi Unit

description: Belmont-Paul Women's Equality National Monument is open Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m.
name: Museum

description:
The park grounds are open from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m., except on New Year's Day, the Birthday of Martin Luther King, Jr, Washington's Birthday, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day. During the off-season [October-March annually], the grounds are open daily from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m., except for the holidays listed above.

Access into the reconstructed fort on a separate schedule—see the event calendar for hours of operation on scheduled plaza access days.
name: Bent's Old Fort National Historic Site

description: Bering Land Bridge Preserve is open 24/7 year round. Due to its remote location accessing the preserve can be challenging, costly and time consuming.
name: Bering Land Bridge National Preserve

description: Big Bend National Park is always open. You can enter and exit at any time of the day or night. Park entrances are open 24 hours daily, all year.
name: Big Bend National Park

description: The preserve is open 24 hours a day 365 days a year.  The Oasis Visitor Center and the Nathaniel P. Reed Visitor Center are closed on December 25th. No fee is charged for access to the preserve.
name: Big Cypress National Preserve

description: The grounds and trails at Big Hole National Battlefield are open daily from sunrise to sunset.
name: Big Hole National Battlefield

description: The park is always open.
name: Park Operating Hours

description: Big Thicket National Preserve is open 24 hours. To camp in the preserve, you need a backcountry camping permit, available from the visitor center.
name: All Park Hours

description:
The park is open every day. 

The Yellowtail Dam Visitor Center in Fort Smith, MT is only open in the summer from Memorial Day to Labor Day. 

The Cal S. Taggart visitor center in Lovell, WY operates on seasonal hours;
Summer Hours (Memorial Day to Labor Day): 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM; Seven days a week.
Winter Hours (Labor Day to Memorial Day): 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM; Tuesday through Saturday. Closed Sundays and Mondays.

description: The Cal Taggart Visitor Center is opened Tuesday-Saturday from 8:30am-4:30pm.

(401) 725-8638

Visitor centers and other facilities have hours and seasons that vary. Most picnic areas, campgrounds, and visitor centers are closed from the beginning of November until early May.

description:

Booker T. Washington National Monument will be closed for Thanksgiving Day, December 24 after 2 p.m., December 25 all day, and January 1 all day. The park may close early on December 31. The park closes sometimes due to inclement weather. Call the park at 540-682-0173 for more information.

description: Boston African American National Historic Site rangers offer tours of the Black Heritage Trail seasonally. Please check the park calendar for available tours and programs.

description:

Visit bostonharborislands.org to plan your trip.
name: Boston Harbor Islands

Only 25 people are allowed in the Monument at a time. Last climb is 30 minutes before closure.

Be advised: The Monument may close for climbing due to weather conditions and/or other safety considerations.
name: Bunker Hill Monument

The Old South Meeting House is a Boston National Historical Park partner site operated by Revolutionary Spaces.

For more visitor information, please visit the Revolutionary Spaces website.
name: Old South Meeting House

The Great Hall is operated as a historic site by the National Park Service in partnership with the City of Boston. Faneuil Hall is owned and managed by the City of Boston. The Great Hall is closed to public visitation when City-permitted events are scheduled.

Visitors can find maps and other visiting materials in the Great Hall.

description: The grounds of the Brices Cross Roads National Battlefield Site are open sunrise to sunset everyday of the year.

description: Bryce Canyon National Park is open 365 days a year, 24 hours a day.

description:
Buck Island Reef National Monument is open year-round, every day of the week. The current operating hours are 7:00 am to 5:00 pm.

There is no overnight camping on the island; the park is closed at night to protect nesting sea turtles.

Camping is not allowed on the island.
name: Buck Island Reef National Monument

description:
Buck Island Reef National Monument is open year-round, every day of the week. The current operating hours are 7:00 am to 5:00 pm.

There is no overnight camping on the island; the park is closed at night to protect nesting sea turtles.

Camping is not allowed on the island.
name: Buck Island Reef National Monument

description: Buffalo National River is 135 miles long. The park is open 24 hours.
name: Buffalo National River

description: The Butterfield Overland National Historic Trail is not a clearly marked hiking trail. Instead, it is a corridor that passes through communities, urban areas, public lands, and Wilderness. The route travels across a variety of land ownerships and management, including private land. Each location varies as to the hours of operation and access. Please contact individual trail sites before your visit for more information.
name: Butterfield Overland National Historic Trail

The park is open 365 days a year including holidays from 9 am until 5 pm. 
The Bayside Trail and the Oceanside Trail close at 4pm. The tidepools and the Coastal Trail close at 4:30pm.
name: Cabriilo National Monument

description: The California National Historic Trail is not a clearly marked hiking trail. Instead it is a corridor that passes through communities, urban areas, public lands, and Wilderness. The route travels across a variety of land ownerships and management, including private land. Each location varies as to the hours of operation and access. Please contact individual trail sites before your visit for more information.
name: California National Historic Trail

description:
The grounds featuring nearly 5-miles of unpaved trails and dozens of wayside exhibits explaining the significance of Camp Nelson, are open 7-days a week from sunrise to sunset. 

Please see the Visitor Center tab for opening and closing hours, and seasonal changes.
name: Camp Nelson National Monument

description: Canaveral National Seashore is open 7 days a week. Visitor hours are 6:00 am to 8:00 pm. Last entry into the seashore is at 7:00 pm.

name: Seminole Rest Historic Site- Oak Hill

description: The Texas & Pacific Railway Depot, Oakland Plantation and Magnolia Plantation are open Wednesday through Sunday from 9 am to 3:30 pm. Both self-guided and guided tours of the plantation grounds and outbuildings are available. In addition, the park store located in the Oakland Plantation store is open for shopping. The Oakland Plantation Main House is open for self-guided tours only on Saturday and Sunday from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. Closed all federal holidays.
name: Oakland and Magnolia Plantations

description: North Rim Drive, South Rim Drive, 9 overlooks open daily all year round. The Welcome Center is open daily from 8AM to 5PM and closed for Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Years Day. Welcome Center gates open and close daily with regular business hours. No entrance fee for park.

description: Canyonlands National Park is generally open 24 hours a day, year-round.

description: Beaches, parking areas, and trails are open year-round between 6 am and midnight.

description: The grounds of Cape Hatteras National Seashore are always open for visitors, though there are resource closures that seasonally affect different parts of the beach.
name: Park Grounds

This quarter acre of ground marks the approximate site of the first landing of the Jamestown settlers in Virginia.
name: Cape Henry

description: Since there are no roads into Cape Krusenstern National Monument, the visitor center is located at the Northwest Arctic Heritage Center in Kotzebue, AK.  From Kotzebue, visitors will charter an air taxi to transport them into the actual national monument which is open 24/7 every day of the year.
name: Cape Krusenstern National Monument

description: Cape Lookout National Seashore has 3 offshore barrier islands that are open 24 hours and 365 days a year (weather permitting). Operating hours for visitor centers and other services will vary throughout the year.

name: Capitol Reef National Park

description: The water trail is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year! To launch your kayak or other vessel on the trail, access a local boat launch along one of the trail's many tributary rivers and coastlines. Local boat launches, state parks, and other locations have specific operating hours and seasons. Always check weather and conditions before going out on the water - take care during the winter months to dress appropriately for the weather and the possibility of submersion in cold water.

Vehicle access to the Volcano Road and the Visitor Center have separate hours. Please see their individual hours.
name: Capulin Volcano National Monument Trails

description:
Parking lots, grounds, gardens, and trails are open every day, year-round, sunrise to sunset. Occasional closures arise from weather events, check park alerts for more information.

The Sandburg Home, Visitor Center and Farm Area have seasonal hours that vary. 

House Tours are by reservation only through http://www.recreation.gov
name: Park Bookstore and House Tours

description: The park grounds are open 7 days a week sunrise to sunset. The barn and goat area are also open 7 days a week from 10am to 4pm. The Sandburg Home Visitor Center, and Park Store are open Wednesday -Sunday (5 days per week) from 10am to 4pm.
name: Park Grounds

description: Standard hours and days are subject to change on short notice. The park is not accessible on weekends and federal holidays.

description:

The gate closes 15 minutes prior to closing, with access for exiting the park still available on the outgoing side.
name: Operating Hours

description: The Castillo de San Marcos is open to the public 7 days per week, except Thanksgiving Day (fourth Thursday in November) and Christmas Day (December 25). A maximum capacity of 100 visitors in the historic fort at any time will be in effect. First admission is at 9:00 a.m. and last admission is at 5:00 p.m. The Castillo closes and visitors must exit at 5:15 p.m. The park grounds are closed from midnight until 5:30 a.m.
name: Castillo de San Marcos National Monument

description: Castle Clinton is open daily except Thanksgiving day and Christmas Day.
name: Castle Clinton

description: Castle Mountains National Monument is open year-round. There are no facilities in the Monument. Visitor Centers in neighboring Mojave National Preserve provide information and orientation.
name: Castle Mountains

description: Hiking trails and parking areas are open year round, dawn to dusk, but may close as a result of security or safety concerns.  Consult the park's website for updates: http://www.nps.gov/cato/index.htm.  The visitor center, camps, and campgrounds have separate hours.  Please see descriptions for those facilities elsewhere.
name: Catoctin Mountain Park

description: Visitor access, services, and activities vary by season. The road through the park, State Route-148, is open to vehicle access between late-May and mid-October. Road access is snow dependent. Visitor services and facilities vary, visit the Operating Hours and Seasons or Current Conditions pages for more information.
name: Park Hours

description: Public areas administered by the National Park Service— Morning Attack Trails, Claven Lane parking area, Vermont Monument property and parking area, and park headquarters— are open from sunrise to sunset.

description: All hiking trails and archaeological sites are open from 7am until 9:00pm from May 1-October 31. The visitor center is open from 9:00am-5:00pm daily. At 8:30 PM, you must be leaving or if you are camping, you need to be back in the campground.
name: Chaco Culture National Historical Park

description: The Memorial grounds are open to vehicle and foot traffic only when the gates are open.
name: Entrance Gates and Memorial Grounds

description: The park is open 24 hours a day. Services and facilities have separate hours. Please see descriptions of those facilities elsewhere.
name: Channel Islands National Park

description:
The historic home (Visitor Center) is open seasonally. For the 2026 season, the Visitor Center will be open three days a week Friday through Sunday from 9:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m.. The season begins on Friday, May 1, 2026 and ends on Sunday, September 27, 2026.

Park grounds are open Wednesday - Sunday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM. 

The entrance gate to the parking lot will be locked when the grounds are closed.
name: Charles Pinckney National Historic Site

description:
The Young family home, Youngsholm, has reopened after a multi-year restoration. Contact the park by email at chyo_info@nps.gov if you have questions or concerns. The park grounds are accessible sunrise to sunset every day. 

You can view the park calendar to see what events are planned or visit our social media pages to stay up-to-date on current park happenings.
name: Youngsholm

description:
The park lands and waters are open for visitation from 30 minutes before sunrise to 30 minutes after sunset seven days a week.

The Island Ford Visitor Center located in historic Hewlett Lodge is open from 9 am to 5 pm seven days a week except Thanksgiving Day and December 25.
name: Park Hours

description: The towpath is open 365 days a year.
name: C&O Canal Towpath

description:
All Park Hours above refers to the core hours the Chesapeake Bay Program Office is open. This program office manages the National Park Service's responsibilities under the Chesapeake Bay Initiative and the Chesapeake Gateways & Water Trails Network. 

The office core hours are from 9 am to 4 pm Monday through Friday. The office is closed on federal holidays. Quite frequently, the office is open for business at least one hour earlier and one hour later than core hours.
name: NPS Chesapeake Bay Office Hours

description: All units of Chickamauga and Chattanooga National Military Park are open during daylight hours, with the following exceptions: Chickamauga Battlefield Visitor Center and Lookout Mountain Battlefield Visitor Center are currently open 8:30 am to 5 pm, daily. Visitor centers are closed Dec. 25 and Jan. 1. Point Park main entrance opens at 8:30 am, daily. US Hwy 27 Picnic Area, Wilder Brigade Monument, & Signal Mountain restrooms are closed Dec.1 - March 15.
name: Chickamauga and Chattanooga National Military Park

description: Many outdoor areas are available 24 hours a day. Please visit the current conditions page for details.
name: Outdoor Areas

description:
The road, campground, and facilities in the park are open overnight. Gates on the road will not be closed during overnight hours unless an emergency occurs. The park prohibits overnight parking, sleeping in vehicles, and camping outside the campground. 

 The trails stay open for sunrise, sunset, and night sky viewing. Be aware of wildlife and use extreme caution.
name: Chiricahua National Monument Facilities and Scenic Drive

description: The Park is open every day from 8:30 am to 4:30 pm. The park is closed on Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day. All cars must be removed from the parking lot by 4:30 p.m. or they will be towed.
name: Christiansted National Historic Site

description:
The National Reserve is open 365 days a year, 7 days a week, 24 hours a day.  Many roads are not maintained during the winter and may become impassable from late November through March. For current conditions visitors are encouraged to check with the Visitor Center or watch for alerts on our website and social media.

During summer the Visitor Center is open every day 8:00 am to 4:30 pm. During fall/winter the Visitor Center is open 8:00 am to 4:30 pm Tuesday-Saturday, closed Sundays, Mondays, and holidays.
name: City of Rocks National Reserve

description:
The park grounds at Battery Kemble, Battleground National Cemetery, Fort Bayard, Fort Bunker Hill, Fort DeRussy, Fort Reno, Fort Slocum, Fort Stevens, and Fort Totten are managed by Rock Creek Park

These sites are open daily from dawn to dusk.
name: Rock Creek Park sites

description: The park grounds at Fort Carroll, Fort Chaplin, Fort Davis, Fort Dupont, Fort Foote, Fort Greble, Fort Mahan, Fort Ricketts, and Fort Stanton are managed by National Capital Parks — East. These sites are open daily from 6:00 am to 10:00 pm.
name: National Capital Parks — East sites

description: The park grounds at Fort Marcy are managed by George Washington Memorial Parkway. This site is open daily from 6 am to 10 pm.
name: George Washington Memorial Parkway site

description:
The unfurnished first floor of the Clara Barton National Historic Site is open for public visitation Fridays and Saturdays by guided tours at 1:00 pm, 2:00 pm, 3:00pm, and 4:00 pm.
Closed Sun. Dec. 25 & Sun. Jan. 1
name: Clara Barton National Historic Site

description:
The Historic Jamestowne Visitor Center is open. The Historic Jamestowne ENTRANCE GATE opens 8:45 a.m. - 4:30 p.m. daily. Historic Jamestowne Gift Shop is open 9 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. daily.

Glasshouse is open 8:45 a.m. - 4:30 p.m.
name: Historic Jamestowne

description:
The Glasshouse parking area is the first right after you pass through the entrance gate. Park and follow the path to the Glasshouse. Public restrooms are available at this location.

English America's first industry was glassblowing. Here at the Glasshouse, costumed interpreters use the same techniques and tools employed by glassblowers of 1608. Demonstrations are ongoing daily during operating hours.
name: Glasshouse

description: Yorktown Visitor Center is open for gift shop daily 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
name: Yorktown Battlefield

description: The Monument is open 24 hours a day year-round. Rim Rock Road is only closed for poor road conditions (snow, ice, rock fall, etc). For updates on storm or temporary closures call the park hotline at (970) 858-3617 ext 350.
name: Colorado National Monument

description:
Congaree National Park is generally open 24 hours a day year-round. For visitor and staff safety, park operations and/or weather conditions may occasionally result in short term, temporary closures. Please review the park webpage and social media platforms for more information. 

The Harry Hampton Visitor Center is open 9-5, daily. It is closed New Year's Day, President's Day, Columbus Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day. Please check alerts prior to your visit.
name: Congaree Operating Hours (Not Visitor Center Hours)

description: The sites of National Mall and Memorial Parks (other than the Washington Monument) are always open to the public. Restrooms, museums and elevators close in the evenings (check individual sites for hours).
name: Always Open

description: Coronado National Memorial is a day use park. The trails, picnic area, waysides, and Montezuma Pass overlook are open each day from one half hour before sunrise to one half hour after sunset.
name: Park

description:
The visitor center parking lot is open 9:00 am - 5:00 pm. The front gate closes at 5:00 pm. Loop Road closes at 4:30 pm. After hours parking is located 1 mile east at the intersection of Hwy 11 and Hayes Road and is open dawn to dusk.

Visitor Center is open Wed - Sunday 9 - 5.
name: Grounds

description: Crater Lake National Park is open year-round, 24 hours a day. Some roads, trails, and facilities, however, are closed seasonally due to snow. North Entrance Road, and West and East Rim Drives close for the season on November 1 (or earlier if there is significant snowfall). Crews begin removing snow from these roads in April, but opening dates vary. The North Entrance Road and West Rim Drive tend to open in early June. The East Rim Drive typically opens in early July.
name: Crater Lake National Park

description: The park's West Entrance on Oregon Highway 62 is open year round.
name: West Entrance

description: The park's South Entrance on Oregon Highway 62 is open year round.
name: South Entrance

description: The park's North Entrance, off of Oregon Highway 138, is usually open from June through October. It is closed in the winter due to snow. Opening dates vary each year depending on snowfall and road conditions.
name: North Entrance

description: The Loop Road is typically open from mid April through late November. High clearance four-wheel drive is highly recommended on BLM Monument roads and are impassible when snow or water is present.
name: Craters of the Moon National Monument and Preserve

description: The scenic Loop Road is open to vehicles as conditions allow. The Loop Road is generally open to motorized vehicles from late April through late November.
name: Craters of the Moon National Monument

description: The park is open 24 hours a day to pedestrian and cyclist, weather permitting. Some services and facilities have separate hours, however; please see descriptions of those facilities elsewhere.
name: Cumberland Gap National Historical Park

description: The park is open 24 hours a day, all year. The island is only accessible by boat. The visitor center, other facilities, ferry operations, and docks for private boats all have separate hours, however; please see descriptions of those facilities elsewhere.
name: Cumberland Island National Seashore

description: Open 24 hours per day, 365 days a year.
name: Curecanti National Recreation Area

description: Cuyahoga Valley National Park is open everyday, 24 hours a day with the following exceptions: Brandywine Falls, Ledges, Octagon, Kendall Lake, and Kendall Hills close from dusk until morning opening.
name: Cuyahoga Valley National Park

description: The monument is open daily from 10:00 am to 4:00 pm except for the following holidays:  Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving Day and the Friday after, Christmas Eve and Christmas Day, and New Year's Eve and New Year's Day.
name: César E. Chávez National Monument Hours

description: The Wright-Dunbar Interpretive Center is located just west of downtown Dayton on South Williams Street, directly south of Third Street. Interpretive displays, interactive exhibits and the park introductory film are all available at the WDIC. A small gift shop is also on site and is stocked with souvenirs and books related to the Wrights and Paul Laurence Dunbar.
name: Wright-Dunbar Interpretive Center

description: Located on an active U.S. Air Force base (Wright Patterson AFB), the Huffman Prairie Interpretive Center provides visitors with a closer look at the aviation side of the Wright Brothers story.
name: Huffman Prairie Interpretive Center

description: The Paul Laurence Dunbar House Historic Site is currently open Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM.
name: Paul Laurence Dunbar House Hours

description: De Soto National Memorial is open with a Visitor Contact Tent. The park's Visitor Center remains closed due to Hurricane damage. The park cannot offer the movie at this time. Trails are awaiting rehabilitation and access is limited for those who need mobility assistance. There are portable restroom facilities and handwashing stations on site for visitor use.
name: De Soto National Memorial

description: De Soto National Memorial is open seven days a week with exceptions of Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Years Day. The park will also be closed during periods of severe weather like tropical storms and hurricanes.
name: De Soto National Memorial Entrance Gate and Parking Lot Hours

description: The park is open daily all year.

description: Park roads are open 24-hours a day. Most day-use areas within the park are open sunrise to sunset (such as trailhead parking lots, Millbrook Village, and all picnic areas).
name: Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area

description: Denali is open 24-hours a day, although services are limited after-hours (generally after 4 pm in winter or 6 pm in summer). There is no gate at the park entrance. Please note that camping is only possible either in designated campgrounds (with a valid reservation) or in the rest of the park with a free backpacking permit.
name: All Park Hours

description:
In the operating season, Devils Postpile National Monument is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. 

The Monument usually opens in mid-June. By mid to late October, early winter storms in the High Sierra typically begin to bring snow to the area.
name: Devils Postpile National Monument

description: Park roads and trails are open 24 hours/day.  This visitor center bookstore is open on a limited basis. The Belle Fourche River Campground is open mid-May to mid-October.

description: The outdoor areas in the monument is open 24 hours per day. Facilities such as visitor centers and the Quarry Exhibit Hall have specific hours of operation. description: The Dry Tortugas is open 24 hours, 7 days a week. This includes holidays.
name: Dry Tortugas

description: The sites of National Mall and Memorial Parks (other than the Washington Monument) are always open to the public. Restrooms, museums and elevators close in the evenings (check individual sites for hours)

description: NPS Interpretive Rangers and Volunteers on Duty at the Memorial
name: Rangers and Volunteers on Duty

description: The historic Jacob & Sarah Ebey House and the Ebey Blockhouse are open to the public during the summer months, Thursdays through Sundays, from 10am to 4pm.
name: Jacob and Sarah Ebey House

description: The administrative offices located in the Cottage at Sunnyside are open from 9 to 5,  Monday through Friday.
name: Administrative Office

description: The site is temporarily closed for a utilities improvement project.
name: Historic site

description: Park trails are open daily from dawn to dusk.

description:

For the Eisenhower Home tours visit https://www.nps.gov/eise/planyourvisit/eisenhower-farm.htm
name: Updated Hours

description: El Camino Real de Tierra Adentro National Historic Trail is not a clearly marked hiking trail. Instead it is a route that passes through communities, urban areas, public lands, and Wilderness. The route travels across a variety of land ownerships and management, including private land. Beyond the United States, the trail reaches Mexico City, Mexico. Each location varies as to the hours of operations and access. Please contact individual trail sites before your visit for more information.
name: El Camino Real de Tierra Adentro National Historic Trail

description: El Camino Real de los Tejas National Historic Trail is not a clearly marked hiking trail. Instead it is a route that passes through communities, urban areas, public lands, and Wilderness. The route travels across a variety of land ownerships and management, including private land. Each location varies as to the hours of operations and access. Please contact individual trail sites before your visit for more information.
name: El Camino Real de los Tejas National Historic Trail

description:
El Malpais National Monument is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, with the following exceptions:

- Sandstone Bluffs Overlook and the West Zuni-Acoma Trail parking lot are open from sunrise to sunset.
- El Malpais Visitor Center and parking area is open daily from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. Winter hours may vary. The visitor center is closed on Thanksgiving Day, December 25th, and January 1st.
- El Malpais Emergency Operations Center is currently closed to the public.
name: El Malpais National Monument

description:
Winter Hours (October 19, 2025 - May 23, 2026). Visitor center and trails open 5 days/week. Closed Tuesdays and Wednesdays. 

Trail access begins at 9:00 am. Last admittance to the Headland Trail is at 3:00 pm MT. Last admittance to the Inscription Loop Trail is at 4:00 pm MT. There is no access to park trails once the visitor center closes at 5:00 pm.

Closed Thanksgiving Day, December 25, and January 1. 

Summer Hours (May 24, 2026 - October 17, 2026). Park visitor center and trails open 7 days/week.
name: El Morro National Monument

description:
Eleanor Roosevelt's Val-Kill Cottage is open seasonally.
The grounds, trails, and gardens are open every day except Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day.
name: Val-Kill Cottage Tours

description: Park and Val-Kill Cottage tours vary by season. The grounds are open daily, except for New Years Day, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day.

description:

description: Eugene O'Neill National Historic Site must be accessed through a private, gated road owned by a community of neighboring residents. In order to experience Eugene O'Neill National Historic Site, visitors need to make reservations and will be driven by a park shuttle from the Town of Danville, California. You'll receive a tour of Eugene O'Neill's "Tao House" and have time to explore the grounds before returning to Danville on the shuttle.

description:
Fire Island National Seashore encompasses five National Park Service sites on Fire Island, and headquarters and the William Floyd Estate on Long Island. The Fire Island Lighthouse, the William Floyd Estate and Wilderness Visitor Center are open year-round. Watch Hill and Sailors Haven on Fire Island are open seasonally. 

Please be sure to check the hours of operations and the exceptions tab for each site you plan to visit.

description:
From May 1-October 31 First Ladies National Historic Site's Education & Research Center will be open Tuesday-Saturday (closed Sunday and Mondays).

We offer guided tours of the Saxton House at 10:00am, 12:00pm, and 2:00pm. Tours have a capacity of fifteen people per tour, and begin at the Visitor Center. Tickets are on a first come first serve basis.

description:
211 Delaware Street, New Castle DE 19720

The New Castle Court House Museum is open Wednesday - Saturday 10:00AM to 4:00PM, and Sunday 1:30PM to 4:30PM with tours being offered every hour. Please be aware that during tours the New Castle Court House Museum does lock their doors, they re-open the doors at the next available tour time. The New Castle Court House Museum is closed on Tuesdays.
name: New Castle Court House Museum

description:
Old Swedes Historic Site is open on Thursdays, Fridays and Saturdays 10:00am to 3:00pm.

Tours are available by reservation and have a small fee: Adult $12, Student (6-15) $7, under 6 free and members free. Self-Guided Cemetery Tour, $5. 

Old Swedes Historic Site is a partner organization to the National Park Service and does not receive NPS funds. As such, they cannot honor National Parks Annual Passes. You can book your tour by visiting their website.
name: Old Swedes Historic Site

description:
The park is open weekends until October 26, from 10:00am-4:00pm.

description:
The Brandywine Valley is open sunrise to sunset and is free to access.

Guided walking tours are available starting at the John Bell House (43 The Green) Thursday - Saturday at 11:00 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. Guided walking tours are $6.50 per person (children 8 and under are free). Free guided walking tours are available on the first Saturday of every month.
Free guided tours of Legislative Hall are available Thursday - Friday 10:00 a.m. - 1:00 p.m.

description:

Closed Monday and Sunday.

description: Park grounds and trails are open 7 days a week, 365 days a year, from sunrise to sunset. Harsh winter weather may result in delayed openings and closures. Call 814-893-6585 for weather-related updates.

description:

The Visitor Center parking lot gate is locked daily at 4:30 PM. 

Be sure to park at the Hornbek Homestead if you think your hike may run late - this lot is open 24 hours a day as a designated Dark Sky viewing area.

For the daily schedule of what is open, what can be included in your visit, and what tickets are available, please visit the Ford's Theatre Society calendar webpage.

description: Bloody Marsh is roughly where the site of the last battle of the Spanish invasion of Georgia took place on July 7th. Though casualties were relatively light, it would rob the Spanish of their enthusiasm and lead to their abandonment of their campaign. Located 7 miles south from the front gates of the town of Frederica, this relatively minor engagement ended the Spanish threat to the Georgia colonies.
name: Bloody Marsh Site

description: The George Rogers Clark National Historical Park Visitor Center is a great place to start your visit. The visitor center contains a small exhibit area, bookstore, and restrooms. Before going to the Clark Memorial, check-in with a ranger at the visitor center.

description:
The memorial is opened on request. Please check with rangers at the visitor center to ensure that the memorial is open before ascending the 33 steps to the door.

The memorial building was designed by architect Frederic C. Hirons and constructed by the WR Heath company between 1931 and 1933.

Inside the memorial are seven (7) murals by artist Ezra Winter, a bas-relief by Joseph Kiselewski, and a statue of George Rogers Clark by Hermon Atkins MacNeil.
name: Memorial Building

The East Entrance at Desert View - 32 miles east of the village (accessed via US 89 to Cameron, AZ, then west on SR 64, usually has shorter wait times.

A stop at Desert View provides spectacular first views of Grand Canyon and Colorado River. Most Desert View services are open for visitors. Desert View Campground will be open for the 2025 season between April 12 and October 12. 2025. Reservations are required.
